parsed_evaluation.task_summary: Rank Japan, United States, India, and Brazil by GDP per capita and compute the ratio of the highest to the lowest.

rubrics.description: Use a single, clearly specified GDP per capita dataset/year consistently for all four countries.
rubrics.criterion: The agent's trace/output names one data source and a specific year, specifies whether figures are nominal USD or PPP, and applies the same source/year/measure to Japan, United States, India, and Brazil.

rubrics.description: Provide the GDP per capita values for all four countries from the specified source/year.
rubrics.criterion: The execution trace or final output includes numeric GDP per capita values for Japan, United States, India, and Brazil that correspond to the stated source/year/measure.

rubrics.description: Rank the four countries correctly by GDP per capita and identify the extremes.
rubrics.criterion: The agent's final output presents a complete rank order of the four countries that is consistent with the provided values and correctly identifies which country is highest and which is lowest.

rubrics.description: Compute the ratio of highest to lowest GDP per capita using the same values.
rubrics.criterion: The final output includes a numeric ratio equal to (highest GDP per capita)/(lowest GDP per capita) calculated from the provided values, within 1% rounding tolerance.

task_objective: Rank Japan, United States, India, and Brazil by GDP per capita. What is the ratio between the highest and lowest?
task_summary: Rank Japan, United States, India, and Brazil by GDP per capita and compute the ratio of the highest to the lowest.
